mnemonics.title: MONOLITHIC KERNEL FEATURES
mnemonics.content: MONO = ONE - All services in single address space: PROCESS management, MEMORY management, FILE system, DEVICE drivers, SECURITY - all tightly coupled.

mnemonics.title: MICROKERNEL MINIMUM
mnemonics.content: MICRO = SMALL MINIMUM: Only IPC, BASIC scheduling, MINIMAL memory management in kernel; everything else runs as USER servers for RELIABILITY.

mnemonics.title: SYSTEM CALL STEPS
mnemonics.content: SAVE state, TRAP to kernel, VERIFY request, EXECUTE service, RESTORE state, RETURN to user - SAFELY switch modes.

mnemonics.title: OS LAYERS HIERARCHY
mnemonics.content: HARDWARE at bottom, then KERNEL services, then SYSTEM CALLS, then SHELL/UI, then USER APPLICATIONS - each layer ABSTRACTS the one below.

acronyms.usage: Library-level functions that programs use to request OS services; may internally use system calls.
Mechanisms like pipes, sockets, message queues that allow processes to communicate.
Dynamically loadable code that extends kernel functionality without reboot (e.g., device drivers in Linux).
Standard API for Unix-like systems ensuring portability across different OS implementations.
Abstraction layer in Linux allowing different file systems to work through common interface.
Defines how programs interact with OS at binary level, including system call conventions.

visualTips.visualization: Draw two boxes - KERNEL SPACE (privileged, full hardware access) and USER SPACE (restricted). Show system calls as ARROWS crossing from user to kernel. Emphasize this is a PROTECTION BOUNDARY.
Draw INVERTED PYRAMID with HARDWARE at bottom, then KERNEL, then SYSTEM CALLS, then SHELL, then APPS at top. Draw arrows showing how each layer uses services FROM BELOW only.

Side-by-side diagrams: MONOLITHIC = single large block (kernel) with all components inside. MICROKERNEL = small central block with separate boxes (servers) surrounding it, connected by message arrows.
Draw USER PROGRAM → (calls library) → LIBRARY API → (traps/interrupt) → KERNEL → (executes) → returns to USER. Show mode switch from USER MODE to KERNEL MODE.

analogies.analogy: Just as city government provides essential services (water, electricity, law enforcement) to citizens, the kernel provides essential services (memory, CPU, I/O) to applications. Citizens can't access water treatment plants directly - they go through city services.
Like citizens must visit government offices for official work, programs must use system calls to access protected resources. You can't just walk into a bank vault - you go through proper channels.

Each service (file system, networking) is like an independent contractor. If one quits or fails, others keep working. Monolithic is like all employees in one building - if the building collapses, everything stops.
Hardware is like raw ingredients (farmer), kernel is kitchen chef, system calls are waiters, shell is menu, applications are dishes served to customers. Each layer transforms and adds value.
